items.title: Analysis tab — user-customisable sub-tabs binding chart components
items.description: Panel registry + multi-tab over it (render only the active tab's panels) + a Settings tab-layout editor; schema migration 54→55 backfills default named tabs. Subsumes the collapsed-chart per-packet setOption concern. (This is the worked Lapse-2 exhibit from the 2026-06-01 RCA: it stood documented as an open Future project for two days after shipping.)

items.title: Consolidate the four recursive-CTE implementations
items.description: Extract one `_build_lineage_cte(root_predicate, max_depth)` helper; the four current call sites (tree_engine.fetch_lineage, tree_engine.build_selection_cte, tree_queries.get_lineage_cte, tree_dsl.SubtreeSelection.to_cte) delegate to it. Closes the hole where a bug-fix to one variant never propagates to the others. Trigger: backend architectural bandwidth.

items.title: Single CTE per pipeline run
items.description: PipelineExecutor.run loops over context_ids issuing one CTE round-trip per id; replace with one CTE keyed off `WHERE card_source.card_id IN (:context_ids)`, first-seen-wins becoming MIN(depth) GROUP BY. Observable behaviour identical; latency drops linearly with context count. Trigger: pairs with 30d — do 30d first, then this becomes a one-liner.

items.title: Navigation-during-range-query perf (regime B)
items.description: During a live range query the median frame ran ~47ms (~3.3× the 60fps budget) — the user-perceived sluggishness, substantiated 2026-05-29. Umbrella item over three levers (RB-1/RB-2/RB-3, tracked as child items). RB-1 and RB-2 shipped; RB-3 was measured and dropped. Residual: whether the regime-B frame cost is fully retired wants a re-profile (ADR-0009) before the win is claimed. Trigger: user-prioritised.

items.title: RB-1 — App ↔ engine-metrics decouple
items.description: App re-rendered the whole tree on every metric tick (531×); Toolbar now self-sources status/metrics → 0. Cleared the metric-driven whole-tree re-render (~1s). Modest frame impact alone; the big costs were RB-2/RB-3.

items.title: RB-3 — packet-receive-path chunking
items.description: Hypothesised chunking lever for the packet-receive path. Instrumented and re-measured on current main: the onAnalysisUpdate handler is ~99ms total (p50 0.2ms/packet), NOT the ~2.35s the pre-refactor audit attributed to it. No bottleneck, no chunking lever to build — dropped. The named Effect-TS reserve trigger did not fire.

items.title: Polymorphic chart renderer abstraction
items.description: Introduce a ChartRenderer Port-equivalent (ADR-0003) so the chart surface can host alternative backends (custom canvas, D3+canvas, Plotly) without rewriting every consumer — motivated by ECharts' destroy-and-recreate-every-Rect heatmap redraw. Open design questions (seam location, Port surface, theme-substrate coupling, bundle weight) are substantial enough to warrant a dedicated design note before implementation; not blocking any current arc. Trigger: user prioritisation.
